title: Isoloma rhynchocarpum
taxonomy: Isoloma rhynchocarpum is a species of plant within the genus Isoloma. It belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae, a family of ferns. The family Hymenophyllaceae is part of the larger class Polypodiopsida, which includes various types of ferns. Further classification includes the order Hymenophyllales, and the class Polypodiopsida falls under the division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and evolutionary relationships with other fern species.

intro: Isoloma rhynchocarpum is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the staff vine or staff tree. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Isoloma rhynchocarpum include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Isoloma rhynchocarpum is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems.
